scope_note: QA Witt Tower Orbit Refinement cert [389]. Primary sources: Wall (1960) doi.org/10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano tower pi(p^k)=p^(k-1)*pi(p)); Serre (1979) doi.org/10.1007/978-1-4757-5673-9 (local fields, Witt vectors §II.4); Neukirch (1999) ISBN 978-3-540-65399-8 (p-adic structure §II.3); Ireland & Rosen (1990) ISBN 978-0-387-97329-6 Ch.7. CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): for sigma(a,b)=(a+b,a) on Z[phi]/(p^2), the periods are exactly {1} union Periods_nt(p) union p*Periods_nt(p) where Periods_nt(p) = non-trivial periods at level p. Corollary: inert and split-equal primes give 3 tiers at level p^2; split-unequal primes give 5 tiers — a finer discriminant than the level-p test alone. The Witt multiplier is always exactly p (not p+-1). Period-1 (zero) does NOT lift to period-p. Hecke interpretation: the k -> p*k map on periods is the local level-raising operator; k-period orbits at level p^2 not in p*Periods_nt are the old-part (embedded from level p); p*k orbits are the new-part in the Witt sense. Checks WITT_REFINEMENT_LAW/TIER_COUNTS/WITT_MULTIPLIER_IS_P/ZERO_DOES_NOT_LIFT/ORBIT_COUNTS_P4; 5 PASS + 6 fixtures (5 PASS, 1 FAIL). Extends certs [387] (Witt carry for p=3) and [388] (split prime orbit geometry at level p). Derived 2026-06-11.
